sn35.24:1.1: “Monks, I will teach you the All as a phenomenon to be abandoned.
sn35.24:1.2: Listen & pay close attention. I will speak.” “As you say, lord,” the monks responded to him.
sn35.24:1.3: The Blessed One said, “And which All is a phenomenon to be abandoned?
sn35.24:1.4: The eye is to be abandoned. Forms are to be abandoned. Eye-consciousness is to be abandoned. Eye-contact is to be abandoned. And whatever there is that arises in dependence on eye-contact—experienced as pleasure, pain or neither-pleasure-nor-pain—that too is to be abandoned.
sn35.24:1.5: “The ear is to be abandoned. Sounds are to be abandoned… “The nose is to be abandoned. Aromas are to be abandoned…
sn35.24:1.6: “The tongue is to be abandoned. Flavors are to be abandoned…
sn35.24:1.7: “The body is to be abandoned. Tactile sensations are to be abandoned…
sn35.24:1.8: “The intellect is to be abandoned. Ideas are to be abandoned. Intellect-consciousness is to be abandoned. Intellect-contact is to be abandoned. And whatever there is that arises in dependence on intellect-contact—experienced as pleasure, pain or neither-pleasure-nor-pain—that too is to be abandoned.
sn35.24:1.9: “This is called the All as a phenomenon to be abandoned.”
